orgPolicy.title: FY27 Offer Calculators Updated
orgPolicy.summary: Updated offer calculators are live for FY27. L6 variable comp increased to 25% (from 20%). L1–L5 require manual variable entry.
bullets: L6: variable target now 25% (was 20%) — straightforward, pre-populated
L1–L5: manual entry required — hourly = $1,250 flat; salary L1–L3 5%, L4 7.5%, L5 10%
Files saved in shared Drive folder (link shared in #invite-team)

tp.point: Use updated FY27 offer calculators for all new offers — L1–L5 require manual variable entry.

orgPolicy.title: Comp Sheets Down — Interim Workaround
orgPolicy.summary: Comp spreadsheets are currently inaccessible. Recruiters can view comp ranges directly in Greenhouse job details, or ping their PE for the master sheet.
bullets: Navigate to job details in Greenhouse → comp range populates based on primary location and job profile
PE master spreadsheet also available — ping your PE directly for comp info
Do NOT share PE master spreadsheet directly with recruiters

tp.point: Comp sheets are down — use Greenhouse job details for comp ranges, or ping your PE directly.

orgPolicy.title: Scorecard Policy: Human-Written Summaries Required for HC
orgPolicy.summary: Hiring Committee is flagging scorecards containing only AI/BrightHire transcripts. A human-authored summary is required — consistently missing, causing back-and-forth.
bullets: Every scorecard needs a human-written synthesis — the recruiter or interviewer's own take
AI/BrightHire notes alone are NOT sufficient and will be flagged in HC
Do not submit to HC if any scorecard is still missing its human summary — a delay is better than an incomplete submission
Recruiters must hold their interviewers accountable to this standard

tp.point: Every scorecard must have a human-written summary — AI transcription alone will be flagged in HC. Do not submit incomplete packets.

orgPolicy.title: VMA Evolution — Launching August 3
orgPolicy.summary: VMA program is being refreshed and relaunched August 3 with updated questions, calibrated rubric, new AI mindset component, and formal performance tie-in.
bullets: New AI mindset component: assesses adaptability/learning orientation — not technical skills
Structured nomination flow replacing self-volunteering; Invite Ops manages the bench
Performance tie-in: active VMA participation named formal contribution for L3–L6 Gusties
Engineering: standalone VMAs reintroduced immediately; CX: targeting Q2 for full reintroduction
Celena joining next PE meeting to walk through nomination process + I&O partnership
Comms went to Gusto Admins June 12 — PEs have been briefed

tp.point: VMA Evolution launches Aug 3 — updated process, new AI mindset component, performance tie-in. More from your PE after Celena's briefing next week.

orgPolicy.title: Cycle 2 Planning Tracker — 'What is this?' + 'Done when...' Due EOD June 13
orgPolicy.summary: Cycle 2 (June 15 – July 31) is live. Six initiatives are logged but several are missing key fields. All DRIs must complete their initiative pages by EOD Friday.
bullets: 'What is this?' — 2–4 sentence plain-language description for someone with zero context
'Done when...' — 3–6 checkboxes defining what completion looks like
Use the Invite Cycle Planner Claude skill or edit directly in Notion
Cycle 2 priorities surface to People Staff and XFN partners — write with outside audience in mind

orgPolicy.title: Mid-Quarter Check-Ins — Next Week
orgPolicy.summary: PEs will conduct light performance check-ins with each direct report next week. No prep needed from recruiters.
bullets: Light check-in — not a full review
No prep required from recruiters

tp.point: Expect a light mid-quarter check-in from your PE next week — no prep needed.

orgPolicy.title: Gusto Closed June 19 — Juneteenth
orgPolicy.summary: Gusto is closed Friday June 19 for Juneteenth. Plan candidate and hiring workflows accordingly.
bullets: No Gusto work expected June 19 — factor into offer timelines and interview scheduling

tp.point: Gusto closed Friday June 19 — Juneteenth. Plan your week accordingly.

items.heading: Gabe Cadavillo Leaving Gusto
bullets: Gabe is departing at end of this week after 4+ years as a consistent, thoughtful contributor
Leaving in great standing — Lisa shared the news and appreciation in #invite-team June 11

items.heading: Greenhouse Open for Ops Conference — Key Takeaways
bullets: Theme: 'Built to Flex — designing TA ops for any hiring environment'
Design for flexibility: build good frameworks, let recruiters customize within them; don't solve everything with one solution
Enablement must be ongoing: gamify it, workshop it, hackathon it — not a one-time training
Data rethink: data isn't dirty, it's unstructured — structure/define/audit your sources
Metrics rethink: Time to Fill is lagging — focus on reschedule rate, conversion, time to feedback; Quality of Hire belongs to HM; activity is a dev tool not a management device
Human-in-the-loop tiers: 100% human (judgment/relationships), AI-assisted (screening/scheduling/synthesis), fully automated (status updates/workflows)
GH products to watch: Fraud detection, Notetaker (BrightHire alt), Analytics, Ezra (AI screening)

items.heading: Requisition Process Review — Saumya Chopra at Next PE Meeting
bullets: Saumya (People Tech) joining PE meeting next Wednesday
Topics: multi-step approval process pain points + requisition routing by job details in Workday
Goal: hear directly from PEs to prioritize People Tech improvements
